Red Light Green Light

DejaGnu Summary Report

Original Report (sha3-256: 179897e78b1fcfdcbe7901f84c2d240855f4c4979dde5fdfca609a91121c8ea6)
project libffi
sha 132fb82
cc ${CC}
host armv7a-linux-androideabi
RESULT ID
XFAIL libffi.threads/tsan.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.threads/tsan.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}

    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.complex/complex_float.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.complex/complex_double.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test

    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",

    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",

XFAIL libffi.complex/cls_complex_double.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.closures/stret_large2.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.closures/stret_large2.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

XFAIL libffi.closures/nested_struct6.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.closures/nested_struct1.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.closures/nested_struct.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test

    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.closures/cls_uchar.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.closures/cls_struct_va1.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.closures/cls_struct_va1.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}

    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",

    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.closures/cls_double.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.closures/cls_double.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.closures/cls_double.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.closures/cls_align_longdouble_split2.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.closures/cls_align_longdouble_split.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

XFAIL libffi.closures/cls_6byte.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.closures/cls_3byte1.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

XFAIL libffi.closures/cls_16byte.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.closures/closure_fn0.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.call/x32.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test

    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.call/struct_return_2H.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.call/struct_return_2H.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}

    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.call/struct1.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}

XFAIL libffi.call/return_ll1.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.call/return_ll1.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}

    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.call/return_dbl.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.call/return_dbl.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.call/return_dbl.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}

    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.call/i128-1.c -W -Wall -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.call/callback3.c -W -Wall -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}

    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=77 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=77 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=77 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=76 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=69 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=68 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=52 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=52 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=52 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test

    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=36 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=36 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=36 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=35 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=28 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=27 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=11 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=11 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-callback.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=11 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test

    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=76 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=76 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=76 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=75 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=68 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=67 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=51 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=51 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=51 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test

    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=35 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=35 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=35 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=34 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=27 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=26 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=10 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test 
 commit 186afacbce5ff5cb0ef5c77c02fed403801eacf7 
 Author: Anthony Green <green@moxielogic.com>
Date:   Fri Jun 19 22:19:59 2026 -0400
    Narrow the Android baseline to aarch64/armv7 only
    
    The blanket `^.*android.*` execution-test and tsan XFAILs masked *every* Android
    execution failure, including x86_64 and i686 -- both of which now run natively
    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128
    long double support; that ABI bug was partly hidden by exactly this broad rule).
    
    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com> 
 {
    "report": "dejagnu",
    "result": "FAIL",
    "host": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "target": "armv7a-unknown-linux-androideabi",
    "id": "libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=10 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O2 execution test",
    "project": "libffi",
    "sha": "132fb82",
    "cc": "${CC}",
    "host": "armv7a-linux-androideabi"
}
XFAIL libffi.bhaible/test-call.c -W -Wall -DDGTEST=10 -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-uninitialized -O0 execution test

    on the CI host and pass all tests (x86_64 after libffi gained IEEE binary128

    Restrict the baseline to the aarch64/armv7 hosts, which run statically under
    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
    again so regressions are caught instead of silently tolerated.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

    qemu-user and still fail broadly. x86_64/i686-android failures are now visible
